user_prompt:
all 4 actually:   1. Plugin schema as a validated contract (was prose, becomes a seam)

  - Files: new schemas/plugin.schema.json, schemas/marketplace.schema.json, schemas/skill.schema.json; new bin/check-plugins + .github/workflows/validate.yml. Affects:
  every existing plugin (read-only — they have to pass).
  - Problem: "What is a valid plugin?" lives in CONTRIBUTING.md as English. Twenty-something rules (six skills with these section names, two agents with this frontmatter
  shape, one command with description + argument-hint, plugin.json keys, etc.) re-instantiate themselves in every contributor's head every time someone adds a plugin.
  There's already one drift on disk (railway security-reviewer's tools line). No CI, no Makefile, no script — correctness is per-PR eyeball.
  - Deletion test: delete the schema files in a hypothetical future. Where does complexity reappear? In 209 SKILL/agent/command/plugin.json files, each needing reviewer
  attention. Complexity concentrates into the schema layer — passes the deletion test.
  - Solution: Schemas as the canonical contract. One script walks the marketplace, validates every plugin.json + every SKILL.md frontmatter + every required section +
  every agent frontmatter. CI fails on drift. README/CONTRIBUTING prose stays (humans read it), but the rule is machine-anchored.
  - Benefits:
    - Locality: every "what shape must a plugin take?" rule lives in one place. Today it's spread between CONTRIBUTING.md, the cloud-aws gold-standard exemplar, and
  reviewer memory.
    - Leverage: every PR and every new provider benefits. Adding plugin #20 is gated on bin/check-plugins passing instead of on a maintainer noticing.
    - Test surface: drift cases become red unit tests. tests/fixtures/missing-bash-tools/ → red. tests/fixtures/missing-verification-checklist/ → red. The interface (the
  schema) IS the test surface.

  2. marketplace.json + plugin.json deduplication

  - Files: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (top-level index) and cloud-*/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (×19). Both carry name, description, version.
  - Problem: Same fields, two files, no enforcement. Today they're aligned (all 0.1.0); tomorrow someone bumps one and forgets the other. The index is a derived view, but
  it's hand-maintained.
  - Deletion test: delete marketplace.json. Could you reconstruct it by globbing cloud-*/.claude-plugin/plugin.json? Yes — every field exists; source is just the directory
   name. So marketplace.json is a derived module masquerading as a primary one — currently shallow.
  - Solution: Either (a) regenerate marketplace.json from plugin.json files via bin/regen-marketplace (the script becomes the seam), or (b) keep dual files but make
  Candidate 1's CI fail on drift. (a) is deeper; (b) is cheaper.
  - Benefits:
    - Locality: plugin metadata lives in ONE file per plugin. The index becomes deterministic output.
    - Leverage: bumping a plugin version is one edit, not two.

  3. Architect-agent's well-arc
